name: VQL
summary: Analyze a screenshot into a structured scene (regions/objects with geometry, dominant colours, window title, scene class) through vql:// routes — Wayland-proof visual grounding that reads pixels, not the window manager.
description: VQL (Visual Query Language) turns a screenshot into a structured program of regions and objects. This connector serves that analysis as vql:// URI routes so a urirun node, flow, or readiness kernel can compose visual understanding with capture (kvm) and decision (urivision) in a resolve-first control plane. Because it reads pixels, it grounds UI targets on GNOME-Wayland where window enumeration is blocked by the OS. Read-only analysis runs in-process; vql is imported lazily so the connector stays light (no heavy deps on the enumeration path).
useCases: Ground a UI target by text on Wayland without a window list (image/query/find).
Turn a captured screen into a structured scene for a planner (image/query/analyze).
Diagnose whether a capture is usable (non-blank) before analysing it.
Feed regions to a decision layer (urivision) or a click point to kvm HID.
